notes: In Sumerian tradition the first city; cult center of Enki/Ea
Biblical Erech; type-site of the Uruk period; cult center of Inanna/An
Cult center of Nanna/Sin; capital of the Third Dynasty of Ur
Religious center of Enlil; conferred legitimacy on Sumerian kings but never a political capital
City of the Lagash state; the state's administrative-religious capital Girsu (Telloh) is the separate site girsu
Administrative-religious capital of the Lagash state; Gudea's seat
Rival of Lagash; under Lugalzagesi briefly unified Sumer before Sargon
Home of the conqueror Lugalannemundu in later tradition
Home of Ziusudra the flood hero; source of an early tablet archive
Cult center of the sun god Utu; rival of Isin in the Isin-Larsa period
Seat of two dynasties; first rose as Ur III collapsed
Seat of the first post-Flood dynasty; the title king of Kish implied hegemony over Sumer
Capital of the Akkadian Empire; archaeological site never securely located (likely near the Tigris-Diyala confluence)
Cult center of the sun god Shamash; long-lived Ebabbar temple archive
Capital of the Old Babylonian and Neo-Babylonian empires; carries the regional timeline furthest forward
Planned circular city on the middle Euphrates; razed by Hammurabi
